comments.author: melbkiwi
comments.body: Interesting idea, but this page needs actual proof, not just AI/trading buzzwords. If you’re saying it was built by a licensed finance broker and tested live, show who that is, show the licence details, and show real verified results with drawdown and risk controls. A $1 price tag doesn’t remove the need for credibility.

replies.author: Jabba_au
replies.body: Happy ti jump on a voice call and prove my credintials

replies.author: melbkiwi
replies.body: A phone call proves nothing. Anyone can talk confidently for 10 minutes and claim anything they like. Credentials are documents, licence numbers, registrations, verifiable track records, and independently checkable results, not “trust me bro on Zoom.“

replies.author: Jabba_au
replies.body:
Im based in australia. Happy to do zoom and provide all my acl numbers.

Would love to see yours :)

replies.author: Jabba_au
replies.body:
Thats what i thought. Just trolling and backpeddling when offering to show credentials.

Pathetic tbh

comments.author: RetrieverSoul
comments.body: Ohhhhhkay, for anyone not wanting an unknown ai trading bot, there are indicators that you can look at to make trading easier without dealing with unknown source code. If you use Tradingview, search for OneShotAlgo. This isn't a bot. It's just a signal indicator, although the signals it gives are precise. Tells you right on the chart what you should do
